summary.intent: Fix GitHub issue #427 in the moltis repo where the agent was confused about the `node` parameter in the `exec` tool, causing repeated failures when no remote nodes were configured.
summary.outcome: Implemented two defensive fixes in exec.rs: filtering empty/whitespace node values and guarding node routing behind a has_connected_nodes() check. Added 4 new tests, verified clean compile/clippy/fmt, and committed and pushed the changes.

repo: Tool schemas are built dynamically per-turn via list_schemas() -> parameters_schema(), so runtime state like has_connected_nodes() is reflected immediately in schema output.
The node parameter visibility in the schema (exec.rs ~line 321) and the NODE_ROUTING_GUIDANCE in the system prompt (prompt.rs ~line 492-495) are two separate code paths that must stay in sync.
node_provider is always wired up in server.rs (~line 3597-3606); actual node availability is determined by a runtime atomic counter checked by has_connected_nodes().
The exec execution path (~line 360-364) historically read the node param from model output regardless of whether it appeared in the schema, making it vulnerable to model hallucinations.

code.finding: has_connected_nodes() gates node param inclusion in the tool schema
node param was read from model params unconditionally; now filtered for empty/whitespace and guarded by has_connected_nodes()
list_schemas() calls parameters_schema() dynamically each turn, not cached at startup
NODE_ROUTING_GUIDANCE text is injected into system prompt gated on PromptNodesRuntimeContext.has_nodes, a separate check from the tool schema gate

workflow: When a nested brace-editing approach gets messy mid-edit, it's cleaner to revert and use an early-return/guard pattern instead of deep nesting.
Running cargo test -p moltis-tools after edits is the right scope for fast iteration on tool-level changes.
Tests needed anyhow::Result rather than bare Result for async test functions in this codebase.

friction: Initial Edit of exec.rs created malformed brace nesting, requiring a second Edit to restructure with a cleaner guard pattern.
Test compilation failed on first attempt due to using bare Result instead of anyhow::Result in async test functions.
open_items: No guard ensures the system prompt's NODE_ROUTING_GUIDANCE and the tool schema node param are always in sync — they're separate code paths that could diverge.

Less capable models (e.g. Qwen3-Coder-Next-FP8 via OpenRouter) may still hallucinate the node param from training data even when it's absent from the schema; no mitigation beyond the new execution-path guard.
Error message improvement when node resolution fails (to hint at valid values) was mentioned in the plan but not explicitly confirmed as implemented.
